title: ConstructionPlaneAtAngleDefinition Object
description: ConstructionPlaneAtAngleDefinition defines a ConstructionPlane by an angle around a linear entity.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: redefine
methods.description: Redefines the input geometry of the construction plane.

properties.name: angle
properties.description: Returns a Parameter object that controls the value of the angle. You can use properties of the returned Parameter object to modify the angle.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: linearEntity
properties.description: Gets the linear edge, construction line, or sketch line that defines the axis of rotation for the construction plane.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: planarEntity
properties.description: Gets the planar face or construction plane the angle for this construction plane is measured from and is parametrically dependent on.   This property is only valid for construction planes that were created using the API. When an angle construction plane is created using the user interface the plane is not defined by the user and a plane is automatically inferred by Fusion. In this case, this property will return null.

angle.title: ConstructionPlaneAtAngleDefinition.angle Property
angle.description: Returns a Parameter object that controls the value of the angle. You can use properties of the returned Parameter object to modify the angle.

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

planarEntity.title: ConstructionPlaneAtAngleDefinition.planarEntity Property
planarEntity.description: Gets the planar face or construction plane the angle for this construction plane is measured from and is parametrically dependent on. This property is only valid for construction planes that were created using the API. When an angle construction plane is created using the user interface the plane is not defined by the user and a plane is automatically inferred by Fusion. In this case, this property will return null.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.

classType.title: ConstructionPlaneAtAngleDefinition.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

redefine.title: ConstructionPlaneAtAngleDefinition.redefine Method
redefine.description: Redefines the input geometry of the construction plane.
redefine.return_description: Returns true if the redefinition of the plane is successful.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: A ValueInput object that defines the angle at which to create the construction plane

parameters.name: linearEntity
parameters.description: The linear edge, construction line, or sketch line that defines the axis of rotation to measure the angle about

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: A plane, planar face or construction plane the angle of the construction plane is measured from
